parameters.content:
### Daily Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — daily at 8:00. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Read Source Feed

### Read Source Feed

Pulls the RSS feed and emits one item per entry (title, link, content).

Next: Skip Already-Seen Posts

↻ retries 3×/2s

### Skip Already-Seen Posts

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Keep Top Stories

### Keep Top Stories

Caps the stream at 5 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Rewrite for Our Audience (AI)

### Rewrite for Our Audience (AI)

Single fixed LLM step (no tool choice) — cheaper and more predictable than an agent.

Next: Publish to Slack

### Groq Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Rewrite for Our Audience (AI)** as `languageModel`

🔑 Groq API key

### Publish to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

Next: Log Published Posts (Airtable)

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Log Published Posts (Airtable)

airtable — `create` operation against the connected account.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Collect job posts with HTTP / REST API and Airtable 24/7

**Pattern:** Web Scraping · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Collect job posts with HTTP / REST API and Airtable 24/7. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual price monitoring is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule → Read Source Feed → Skip Already-Seen Posts → Keep Top Stories → Rewrite for Our Audience (AI) → Publish to Slack → Log Published Posts (Airtable) → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to web scraping without manual effort
- Anyone automating price monitoring in data
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Responds in seconds
- Standardizes the process every time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Airtable PAT; Groq API key; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your price monitoring process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
